title: oenothera tubicula subsp. strigulosa
intro: Oenothera tubicula subsp. strigulosa, also known as Evening Primrose, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy stems. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA). Consuming Evening Primrose oil or supplements has been associated with various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and relief from symptoms of menopause and premenstrual syndrome (PMS). Evening Primrose is often used in herbal preparations such as capsules, oils, and teas, and can also be used topically as a cream or salve to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
